seed_entries.title: Decision: Long Polling for Dashboard Push Notifications
seed_entries.body:
Decision: Selected approach is long polling with DB check (no external dependencies).

Rationale: Paid Neon tier removes connection/compute concerns. No external service dependency (vs Pusher). Simple implementation (~2 hours). Less than 2 second update latency.

New Endpoint: GET /api/dashboard/wait-for-update?since=<ISO timestamp>. Holds request open for up to 25 seconds. Polls DB every 1.5 seconds for lastSyncedAt changes. Returns immediately when update detected. Client loops: receive response, fetch data, start new long poll.

Alternatives rejected: Pusher (external dependency, $49/mo), WebSockets (complex session management), SSE (limited browser support for auth headers).

assertions.decision_recall_query: What approach was chosen for dashboard push notifications?

seed_entries.title: POLICY DECISION: Never Test in Production - Branch Workflow Reinforced
seed_entries.body:
POLICY: WE DO NOT TEST IN PRODUCTION. This is an absolute rule. Testing happens in Preview environment, never Production.

Branch to Environment Mapping (ENFORCED):
- stable branch -> Production (release-ready code only)
- main branch -> Preview (all development/testing)
- staging branch -> Preview (staging/QA)
- feature/* branches -> Preview (auto-generated preview URL, feature development)

Deployment Methods: Use GitHub Push (Auto-Deploy). Never use Vercel CLI deploy to production. Never push directly to stable without PR review.

assertions.decision_recall_query: What is the policy on testing in production environments?

seed_entries.title: Solution Approach: Context Propagation with Mode Detection
seed_entries.body:
Decision: Option B selected - Context Propagation with Mode Detection.

Evaluated three approaches to bridge the stdio-to-HTTP gap:

Option A: Full GitHub API Migration (HIGH EFFORT) - Replace ALL filesystem and git operations with GitHub REST/GraphQL API. Rejected: Too much effort (6-8 weeks), race conditions, API rate limits.

Option B: Context Propagation with Mode Detection (MEDIUM EFFORT) - Selected. Pass HTTP context to tools via anyio context variables. Tools detect hosted mode and use GitHub API. Best balance of effort vs value.

Option C: Server-Side Clone (LOW EFFORT) - Clone repos to temp directories per-request. Rejected: Doesn't scale, not truly stateless.

Key design: Context Variable Bridge using Python contextvars, Mode Detection via is_hosted_mode() check, GitHub API Layer for read/write, Dual Code Paths per tool.

assertions.decision_recall_query: How does the system bridge the gap between stdio MCP and hosted HTTP mode?

seed_entries.title: Migration Fix Pattern: P3009 Failed Migration Recovery
seed_entries.body:
Incident (2026-01-14): Vercel preview deployment failed with Prisma P3009 error. Migration attempted to add columns that already existed (likely from a prior db push): column graphNodes of relation ConnectedRepo already exists.

Diagnosis: Check migration status with SELECT migration_name, finished_at FROM _prisma_migrations WHERE finished_at IS NULL. Check if columns exist in information_schema.

Fix Applied: Since columns already existed, marked the failed migration as rolled back:
UPDATE _prisma_migrations SET finished_at = started_at + INTERVAL '1 second', applied_steps_count = 1, logs = 'Marked as applied - schema already correct' WHERE migration_name = '20260113000002_add_graph_columns' AND finished_at IS NULL.

assertions.decision_recall_query: How do you recover from a failed Prisma migration P3009 error?

seed_entries.title: DIAGNOSIS: No threads found - Missing automatic sync after repo connection
seed_entries.body:
Root Cause Found: Dashboard shows 'No threads found' because users must manually click Sync Threads after connecting repos, but there is no indication they need to do this.

Data Flow Audit:
- Steps 1-3 (OAuth, Repo Discovery): WORKING
- Step 4 (Connect Repository): WORKING but does NOT trigger sync automatically
- Step 5 (Sync Threads): User must manually navigate to Dashboard and click Sync Threads

The missing automatic sync after repo connection is the root cause. The connect action saves to ConnectedRepo table with enabled=true but does not trigger any thread synchronization.

assertions.decision_recall_query: Why does the dashboard show No threads found after connecting a repo?

seed_entries.title: Root Cause Found: Backfill Script + Delete-All Pattern
seed_entries.body: Root Cause Identified: Found the actual cause of the 3.37GB write transfer. Timeline: Nov 18 - 89 snapshots created across 3 repo syncs. Nov 19 - All 89 records updated by backfill script. The Problem: Each sync uses deleteMany + createMany pattern. Even for first sync (no existing data), deleteMany still executes. 3 syncs times delete plus create equals 6 write operations. 89 snapshots times 40KB equals about 3.6MB per operation.

seed_entries.title: Critical Finding: Migration Table Rewrite Caused 3.37GB Write
seed_entries.body: Root Cause: Migration Table Rewrite. The migration on Nov 19 at 01:27:34 added 5 columns with DEFAULT values to an existing table with 89 rows. When you add NOT NULL columns with DEFAULT values, PostgreSQL rewrites the entire table to populate the defaults. This is a full table copy operation including all data pages, all indexes (2MB), WAL entries, and table metadata.

seed_entries.title: Status Update: Transfer Rate Stabilized at 21.5 MB/day
seed_entries.body: Transfer rate has stabilized at a sustainable level. All three optimization phases are working correctly. The 5GB limit will now last approximately 233 days instead of 1 day - a 233x improvement. Current transfer rate: 37.66 MB over 1.75 days = approximately 21.5 MB/day. Baseline (pre-optimization) was 4800 MB/day. Phase 1+2 target was 53 MB/day, actual is 21.5 MB/day (59% better than target).

assertions.rehydration_query: What was the root cause and resolution for the Neon database transfer spike?

Prisma schema includes SlackWorkspace, SlackThreadMapping, SlackChannelConfig, ProcessedSlackEvent. Critical Gap: No workspace is currently connected. New files: slackChannelManagement.ts for channel create/archive/unarchive, provision-channels route, isManaged migration. Flow: User enables repo -> syncRepoSlackChannel creates wc-<repo-name> channel. User disables repo -> Channel archived (reversible). Bug Fix: Missing OAuth Scopes - conversations.list returned missing_scope error. Root cause: OAuth scopes did not include groups:read for private channels.

Phase 3 (Slash commands: sync, rebuild, help) COMPLETE. Phase 4 (Multi-workspace token service) COMPLETE. Active Work: Phase 5 Legacy Auth Removal - remove all SLACK_BOT_TOKEN env var fallbacks. Current Debugging Issues: Add Entry modal view_submission not reaching Vercel, Close modal MCP success but data not persisted.

assertions.rehydration_query: What is the current state of the Slack integration work?

seed_entries.title: ROOT CAUSE FOUND: NextAuth v4/v5 API mismatch - OAuth now fixed
seed_entries.body: Root Cause Analysis: Why OAuth Redirect Failed. Found three critical failures from mixing v4 and v5 APIs. Failure 1: Wrong Import Source - used next-auth/react (v4 client API) instead of @/lib/auth (v5 server action). Failure 2: Wrong Parameter Name - used callbackUrl (v4) instead of redirectTo (v5). Failure 3: Client Component - signIn must be called from server-side route in v5, not from client component.

seed_entries.title: OAuth Fix: NextAuth v5 API Migration + Remaining Workarounds Analysis
seed_entries.body: Fixed the immediate OAuth failure (UnknownAction error) by migrating from custom routes to NextAuth v5 native signIn API. Root cause: NextAuth v5 removed the /api/auth/signin/{provider} GET endpoint that existed in v4. Our custom routes were redirecting to this non-existent endpoint. Changes: hooks/useOnboardingState.ts updated. 189 lines of encoding/decoding workarounds remain from Cursor's iterations that may need cleanup.

assertions.rehydration_query: What was the root cause of the NextAuth OAuth redirect failure?

seed_entries.title: Closure: All Performance Optimizations Complete
seed_entries.body: All sync performance optimization phases have been implemented and verified. Phase 2: Incremental Snapshot Updates - replaced delete-all/create-all with incremental updates (commit 4092070). Phase 3: Incremental Graph Sync - added fetchBaselineGraphIncremental for SHA-based change detection, performance from 60s (555 fetches) to less than 1s (0 fetches) for unchanged repos (commit 63aa359). Rate Limit Fix - removed purge:true from webhook handler, API calls reduced from 450 per entry to 0 (commit 4b34fab). Additional: Parallelized multi-user sync with Promise.allSettled, single-fetch with broadcast for multi-subscriber repos.

assertions.rehydration_query: What sync performance optimizations were made and what were the results?

seed_entries.title: Postmortem: Prisma Migration Workflow Deviation
seed_entries.body: Fact: The migration failure was caused by workflow deviation and running migrate dev instead of db push.

seed_entries.title: CORRECTED: Root Cause - Index vs Constraint Mismatch
seed_entries.body: Fact: The migration failure was caused by index vs constraint mismatch where db push creates unique constraints as indexes in pg_indexes while migrations check pg_constraint. This supersedes the earlier analysis.

seed_entries.title: Use baseline graph as primary data source for thread parsing
seed_entries.body: Fact: The dashboard should consume the baseline graph instead of parsing markdown files directly.

seed_entries.title: Graph-First Consolidation Complete - ThreadSnapshot Removed
seed_entries.body: Fact: ThreadSnapshot table has been removed and all routes now use the baseline graph directly. This supersedes the earlier proposal to consume graph.

entries.title: Known Issue: Neon Advisory Lock Timeout During Deploy
entries.body: Deployments occasionally fail during prisma migrate deploy with error P1002 (advisory lock timeout). Symptoms: Build log shows 'Timed out trying to acquire a postgres advisory lock', exit code 1, red deployment status. Resolution: Simply redeploy - this is a transient Neon/network issue. Methods: Vercel dashboard Redeploy button, git push empty commit, or vercel --prod CLI. Root Cause: See service-reference-neon thread for detailed analysis.

entries.title: Migration Fix Pattern: P3009 Failed Migration Recovery
entries.body: Vercel preview deployment failed with Prisma P3009 error: migrate found failed migrations in the target database. Root Cause: Migration attempted to add columns that already existed from a prior db push. Fix Applied: Marked the failed migration as rolled back in _prisma_migrations table by setting finished_at and applied_steps_count. This is the standard recovery pattern for P3009 errors.

assertions.cross_thread_query: What happens when a Vercel deployment triggers a Prisma migration that fails? What is the recovery process?

entries.title: Architecture Refactoring Plan: Block Kit Consolidation & Collision Protection
entries.body: Phases 1-10 of original Slack UX Enhancement completed: branch selection UI, branch switch operation, rebuild trigger, migration. Critical Issues Identified: 1) Dual Block Kit Implementation - Python blocks.py and TypeScript slackOutbound.ts can drift. 2) No Collision Protection - multiple users can trigger rebuild simultaneously, no locking mechanism. 3) Rebuild operations trigger GitHub API calls for full thread re-fetch.

entries.title: Closure: All Performance Optimizations Complete
entries.body: All sync performance optimization phases complete. Rate Limit Fix: removed purge:true from webhook handler which was bypassing incremental sync, reducing API calls from 450 per entry to 0. Incremental Graph Sync: SHA-based change detection reduces sync time from 60s to less than 1s for unchanged repos. Parallelized multi-user sync with Promise.allSettled.

assertions.cross_thread_query: How do Slack thread rebuilds interact with the sync system? Can a rebuild trigger rate limit exhaustion?

entries.title: OAuth Fix: NextAuth v5 API Migration + Remaining Workarounds Analysis
entries.body: Fixed the immediate OAuth failure (UnknownAction error) by migrating from custom routes to NextAuth v5 native signIn API. Root cause: NextAuth v5 removed the /api/auth/signin/{provider} GET endpoint that existed in v4. Our custom routes redirected to this non-existent endpoint. 189 lines of encoding/decoding workarounds remain from Cursor's iterations.

entries.title: Phases 1 & 2 Complete - All Blocking Work Deployed
entries.body: All blocking and high-value onboarding work deployed to production. Phase 1 (P0): Fixed IterableList.origin errors, implemented HTTPS auto-provisioning via GitHub API, configured GIT_ASKPASS for automatic authentication, 103 tests passing. Phase 2 (P1): HTTP transport with SSE implemented, daemon management operational, multi-org support with UserOrganization model and auto-sync on login deployed to Vercel production.

assertions.cross_thread_query: What authentication and permission setup does a new user go through, and what are the known failure modes?

entries.title: Cross-reference: Per-thread format enables unified write interface
entries.body: The per-thread graph format from this migration enables the tiered write architecture. Old format (monolithic): graph/baseline/nodes.jsonl (all threads, conflict-prone) and edges.jsonl (must rewrite entire file). New format (per-thread): graph/baseline/threads/<topic>/ with meta.json (single JSON, easy to update), entries.jsonl (append-only), edges.jsonl (per-thread). Direct GitHub API can now update meta.json for status/ball/priority without touching entries or risking conflicts with other threads.

entries.title: Implemented: Tier 1 metadata updates via direct GitHub
entries.body: Fixed /api/dashboard/threads/update route to support per-thread graph format. Per-thread format detection: route checks for graph/baseline/threads/<topic>/meta.json first. Tier 1 metadata updates: status, ball, priority changes update only meta.json (1 file instead of full graph). Backward compatibility: falls back to monolithic format if per-thread not found. Rename limitation: returns 501 for rename in per-thread format (should use MCP).

assertions.cross_thread_query: How does the per-thread graph format enable the unified write interface?
